raw_response:
```python
# The circuit applies X gates to qubits 0, 2, 3 and RY(π/6) to qubit 1
# X gates flip |0⟩ to |1⟩, so qubits 0, 2, 3 should measure as 1
# RY(π/6) creates cos(π/12)|0⟩ + sin(π/12)|1⟩ ≈ 0.966|0⟩ + 0.259|1⟩
# So qubit 1 should measure as 0 with high probability
# Expected dominant outcome: qubit 3, qubit 2, qubit 1, qubit 0 = "1101"

dominant_bitstring = "1101"
assert dominant_bitstring in counts, f"Expected dominant bitstring {dominant_bitstring} not found in results"

dominant_count = counts[dominant_bitstring]
total_other_counts = sum(count for bitstring, count in counts.items() if bitstring != dominant_bitstring)

# Verify the dominant outcome has more counts than all others combined
assert dominant_count > total_other_counts, f"Dominant bitstring {dominant_bitstring} ({dominant_count}) should have more counts than all others combined ({total_other_counts})"

# Verify the dominant outcome represents a significant majority (>60% of shots)
dominant_fraction = dominant_count / shots
assert dominant_fraction > 0.6, f"Dominant bitstring should represent >60% of shots, got {dominant_fraction:.3f}"
```

```python
# The circuit applies X gates to qubits 0, 2, 3 and RY(π/6) to qubit 1
# X gates flip qubits to |1⟩, RY(π/6) creates cos(π/12)|0⟩ + sin(π/12)|1⟩
# cos(π/12) ≈ 0.966, sin(π/12) ≈ 0.259
# So qubit 1 has ~93.3% probability of being 0, ~6.7% probability of being 1
# Expected dominant outcome: "1011" (qubits 3,2,1,0 in that order)

max_count_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)
max_count = counts[max_count_bitstring]
expected_dominant = "1011"

# Verify the expected bitstring is the most frequent
assert max_count_bitstring == expected_dominant

# Verify it appears in at least 80% of shots (well above random chance of 6.25%)
assert max_count >= 0.8 * shots

# Verify no other outcome comes close to the dominant one
sorted_counts = sorted(counts.values(), reverse=True)
if len(sorted_counts) > 1:
    assert sorted_counts[0] > 5 * sorted_counts[1]
```

```python
# The circuit applies X gates to qubits 0, 2, 3 and RY(π/6) to qubit 1
# X gates flip |0⟩ to |1⟩, so qubits 0, 2, 3 will be in |1⟩ state
# RY(π/6) creates superposition: cos(π/12)|0⟩ + sin(π/12)|1⟩
# Since π/12 ≈ 0.26 radians, cos(π/12) ≈ 0.966 and sin(π/12) ≈ 0.259
# The probability of measuring qubit 1 in |0⟩ is cos²(π/12) ≈ 0.933
# The probability of measuring qubit 1 in |1⟩ is sin²(π/12) ≈ 0.067
# Expected dominant outcome: qubit 1 in |0⟩, others in |1⟩ → bitstring "1101"

dominant_bitstring = "1101"
assert dominant_bitstring in counts, f"Expected dominant bitstring {dominant_bitstring} not found in counts"

dominant_count = counts[dominant_bitstring]
total_other_counts = sum(count for bitstring, count in counts.items() if bitstring != dominant_bitstring)

# Verify dominant bitstring has clearly higher frequency than all others combined
assert dominant_count > total_other_counts, f"Dominant bitstring {dominant_bitstring} count ({dominant_count}) should exceed sum of all others ({total_other_counts})"

# Verify dominant bitstring represents a significant majority (at least 80% based on theoretical expectation)
dominant_frequency = dominant_count / shots
assert dominant_frequency > 0.8, f"Dominant bitstring frequency ({dominant_frequency:.3f}) should be > 0.8"
```

```python
# The circuit applies X gates to qubits 0, 2, 3 and RY(π/6) to qubit 1
# X gates flip qubits to |1⟩, RY(π/6) creates cos(π/12)|0⟩ + sin(π/12)|1⟩
# Expected dominant outcome: qubit 1 in |0⟩ state, others in |1⟩ state
# In little-endian bit order: "1101" (qubits 3,2,1,0 = 1,1,0,1)

dominant_bitstring = "1101"
max_count = max(counts.values())
dominant_count = counts.get(dominant_bitstring, 0)

# Verify the expected bitstring is the most frequent
assert dominant_count == max_count, f"Expected {dominant_bitstring} to be dominant, but max count belongs to another bitstring"

# Verify it has clearly higher frequency than others (at least 60% of shots)
assert dominant_count >= 0.6 * shots, f"Dominant bitstring {dominant_bitstring} count {dominant_count} should be at least 60% of {shots} shots"

# Verify the second most common result has significantly lower count
sorted_counts = sorted(counts.values(), reverse=True)
if len(sorted_counts) > 1:
    second_highest = sorted_counts[1]
    assert dominant_count > 2 * second_highest, f"Dominant count {dominant_count} should be more than twice the second highest {second_highest}"
```

```python
# The circuit applies X gates to qubits 0, 2, 3 and RY(π/6) to qubit 1
```
```python
# X gates flip qubits to |1⟩, RY(π/6) creates cos(π/12)|0⟩ + sin(π/12)|1⟩
# cos(π/12) ≈ 0.966, sin(π/12) ≈ 0.259
# So qubit 1 has ~93.3% probability of being 0 and ~6.7% probability of being 1
# Expected dominant outcome: "1011" (qubits 3,2,1,0 in binary string order)

max_count = max(counts.values())
max_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)

# Verify the dominant bitstring is "1011" 
assert max_bitstring == "1011", f"Expected '1011' to be dominant, got '{max_bitstring}'"

# Verify it has significantly higher frequency than others
total_other_counts = sum(count for bitstring, count in counts.items() if bitstring != "1011")
dominant_count = counts.get("1011", 0)

# The dominant outcome should have at least 80% of all measurements
assert dominant_count > 0.8 * shots, f"Dominant outcome has {dominant_count}/{shots} = {dominant_count/shots:.3f}, expected > 0.8"

# The dominant outcome should be at least 10x more frequent than all others combined
if total_other_counts > 0:
    assert dominant_count >= 10 * total_other_counts, f"Dominant count {dominant_count} should be ≥10x other counts {total_other_counts}"
```

```python
# The circuit applies X gates to qubits 0, 2, 3 and RY(π/6) to qubit 1
# Expected dominant state: |1101⟩ (qubit 1 in |1⟩ state with high probability)
# RY(π/6) creates cos(π/12)|0⟩ + sin(π/12)|1⟩ ≈ 0.966|0⟩ + 0.259|1⟩
# So qubit 1 should be in |0⟩ most of the time
expected_dominant = "1011"  # qubits 3,2,1,0 = 1,0,1,1 in little-endian

# Get the count for the expected dominant outcome
dominant_count = counts.get(expected_dominant, 0)

# Check that the dominant outcome has the highest count
max_count = max(counts.values())
assert dominant_count == max_count, f"Expected {expected_dominant} to be most frequent"

# Check that the dominant outcome represents a clear majority (> 80% of shots)
dominant_frequency = dominant_count / shots
assert dominant_frequency > 0.8, f"Expected dominant frequency > 0.8, got {dominant_frequency:.3f}"

# Verify the second most common outcome has significantly lower frequency
sorted_counts = sorted(counts.values(), reverse=True)
if len(sorted_counts) > 1:
    second_highest_frequency = sorted_counts[1] / shots
    assert dominant_frequency > 2 * second_highest_frequency, f"Dominant outcome not clearly higher than others"
```

```python
# The circuit applies X gates to qubits 0, 2, 3 and RY(π/6) to qubit 1
# X gates flip qubits 0, 2, 3 to |1⟩
# RY(π/6) on qubit 1 gives cos(π/12)|0⟩ + sin(π/12)|1⟩
# cos(π/12) ≈ 0.966, sin(π/12) ≈ 0.259
# So qubit 1 is mostly in |0⟩ state
# Expected dominant outcome: qubit 3,2,1,0 = 1,1,0,1 = "1101"

dominant_bitstring = "1101"
assert dominant_bitstring in counts, f"Expected dominant bitstring {dominant_bitstring} not found in results"

dominant_count = counts[dominant_bitstring]
total_other_counts = sum(count for bitstring, count in counts.items() if bitstring != dominant_bitstring)

# The dominant outcome should have significantly higher probability
# With cos²(π/12) ≈ 0.933, we expect roughly 93% of measurements to be "1101"
assert dominant_count > 0.8 * shots, f"Dominant bitstring {dominant_bitstring} frequency too low: {dominant_count}/{shots}"
assert dominant_count > 3 * total_other_counts, f"Dominant bitstring not clearly dominant: {dominant_count} vs {total_other_counts} others"
```

```python
# The circuit applies X gates to qubits 0, 2, 3 and RY(π/6) to qubit 1
# X gates flip qubits to |1⟩, RY(π/6) creates superposition with high probability of |1⟩
# Expected dominant outcome is "1111" (all qubits measured as 1)
max_count = max(counts.values())
dominant_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)

# Verify the dominant outcome is "1111"
assert dominant_bitstring == "1111", f"Expected '1111' to be dominant, got '{dominant_bitstring}'"

# Verify it has significantly higher frequency than average
total_other_counts = sum(count for bitstring, count in counts.items() if bitstring != "1111")
num_other_outcomes = len(counts) - 1

if num_other_outcomes > 0:
    avg_other_count = total_other_counts / num_other_outcomes
    assert counts["1111"] > 3 * avg_other_count, f"'1111' count {counts['1111']} should be much higher than average of others {avg_other_count:.1f}"

# Verify "1111" has majority of measurements
assert counts["1111"] > shots / 2, f"'1111' should have majority with {counts['1111']}/{shots} measurements"
```

```python
# The circuit applies X gates to qubits 0, 2, 3 and RY(pi/6) to qubit 1
# X gates flip |0⟩ to |1⟩, RY(pi/6) creates superposition with high probability of |1⟩
# Expected dominant state: qubit 0=1, qubit 1≈1 (high prob), qubit 2=1, qubit 3=1
# In bitstring format (q3 q2 q1 q0): "1111" should dominate

# Find the most frequent measurement outcome
max_count = max(counts.values())
dominant_bitstrings = [bitstring for bitstring, count in counts.items() if count == max_count]

# Verify there's a single dominant outcome
assert len(dominant_bitstrings) == 1, f"Expected single dominant outcome, got {len(dominant_bitstrings)}"

dominant_bitstring = dominant_bitstrings[0]

# The dominant outcome should be "1111" (all qubits in |1⟩ state)
assert dominant_bitstring == "1111", f"Expected dominant bitstring '1111', got '{dominant_bitstring}'"

# Verify the dominant outcome has significantly higher frequency than others
dominant_count = counts[dominant_bitstring]
other_counts = [count for bitstring, count in counts.items() if bitstring != dominant_bitstring]

if other_counts:
    max_other_count = max(other_counts)
    # Dominant outcome should have at least 2x more counts than any other outcome
    assert dominant_count >= 2 * max_other_count, f"Dominant outcome count {dominant_count} should be at least 2x higher than max other count {max_other_count}"

# Dominant outcome should represent majority of measurements
assert dominant_count > shots // 2, f"Dominant outcome count {dominant_count} should be more than half of total shots {shots}"
```

```python
# The circuit applies X gates to qubits 0, 2, 3 and RY(pi/6) to qubit 1
# This creates a superposition where qubit 1 has high probability of being |1>
# Expected dominant state: |1111> (all qubits in |1> state)
dominant_state = "1111"

# Verify the dominant state exists and has the highest count
assert dominant_state in counts, f"Expected dominant state {dominant_state} not found in results"
max_count = max(counts.values())
assert counts[dominant_state] == max_count, f"State {dominant_state} is not the most frequent outcome"

# Verify the dominant state has significantly higher frequency than average
total_other_counts = sum(count for state, count in counts.items() if state != dominant_state)
num_other_states = len(counts) - 1
if num_other_states > 0:
    avg_other_count = total_other_counts / num_other_states
    assert counts[dominant_state] > 2 * avg_other_count, f"Dominant state frequency {counts[dominant_state]} is not significantly higher than average of others {avg_other_count}"
```
